characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Action Girl: Probably the most capable fighter in the series. Justified since her military dad taught her. Almighty Janitor: A young adult who works as a nanny with fighting skills and a pretty good ability to save the not always grateful Ross Kids when it's necessary. Birds of a Feather: A platonic version of this with Officer Petey. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The redhead to Emma's blonde & Zuri's brunette. Jessie is not a natural redhead, but she doesact like one. Book Dumb: Depending on the episode. Jessie just graduated high school before the pilot, but doesn't know the answer to a simple fraction problem that Luke had to do. In another episode, she also didn't know if a fraction with a denominator of zero would be undefined.

characters.name: Jessie Prescott
characters.description: Action Girl: Probably the most capable fighter in the series. Justified since her military dad taught her. Almighty Janitor: A young adult who works as a nanny with fighting skills and a pretty good ability to save the not always grateful Ross Kids when it's necessary. Birds of a Feather: A platonic version of this with Officer Petey. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The redhead to Emma's blonde & Zuri's brunette. Jessie is not a natural redhead, but she doesact like one. Book Dumb: Depending on the episode. Jessie just graduated high school before the pilot, but doesn't know the answer to a simple fraction problem that Luke had to do. In another episode, she also didn't know if a fraction with a denominator of zero would be undefined.

characters.name: Emma Ross
characters.description: All Girls Want Bad Boys: In “Badfellas”, Emma develops a crush on Vincent, the classic rebellious bad boy at school. Although their relationship didn’t last long. All Women Love Shoes: Orders forty pairs of shoes in "Green-Eyed Monsters", to name one of the better examples. Aloof Big Sister: To Luke. Bad Taste Call-Out: Emma always has something to say about people's attire... usually to tell them that they made a fashion faux pas. Jessie is her most common victim, as she's happy to point out any part of her nanny's wardrobe that she thinks is in poor taste. This isn't limited to Jessie, though, as anyone who makes what she considers a fashion mistake instantly earns her ire.Emma:(after finding the butler's pants from the seventies)Bell-bottoms, Bertram?! I can't even look at you right now! Big Sister Instinct: To Ravi and Zuri.Not so muchwith Luke.

characters.name: Luke Ross
characters.description: Aesop Amnesia: No matter how many times he learns to work hard in school, he will go back to being a slacker. Even after the finale, Luke promises he will work harder, the first episode ofBunk'dmade it clear Luke does not make good on his promise, as he had to go to summer school. When he visits inBunk'd, we find out he's been getting straight A's. Looks like the lack of siblings did wonders for him. All Men Are Perverts: There's a running gag with him hitting on Jessie… andoccasionally stalking her. Aloof Big Brother: To Ravi (and, to a degree, Zuri) at first, but he's getting better. Ambiguously Brown: His ethnicity isn't confirmed, but there are several hints that he's meant to be Latino. His actor Cameron Boyce is half black and half Jewish. Annoying Younger Sibling: To his older sister Emma who is often annoyed by Luke's habits. He’s also an annoyingoldersibling to Zuri and Ravi at times.

characters.name: Ravi Ross
characters.description: Adorably Precocious Child Alliterative Name:RaviRoss Annoying Younger Sibling: Ends up becoming one by making Luke do intellectual things, or trolling him with intellect. Bestiality Is Depraved:Played for Laughsin "Capture the Nag," where he outright calls Mrs. Kipling his soul mate.Jessie: Okay, wereallyneed to talk about boundaries between you and that lizard. Beware the Nice Ones: When angry, or scared he will often scream in Hindi.

characters.name: Zuri Ross
characters.description: Annoying Younger Sibling: Mostly to Luke, but she also annoys Emma or Ravi on occasion. Blonde, Brunette, Redhead: The brunette to Emma's blonde & Jessie's redhead. Brainy Brunette:Zig-Zagged. She's quite street-smart for her agebut can also be a naive Cloudcuckoolander. Bratty Half-Pint: Moreso in later seasons. Cheerful Child: A very energetic, cheerful, and playful little girl.

characters.name: Bertram Winkle
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: Tends to take the most abuse from other characters. Camp Straight: Especially in "Make New Friends but Hide the Old”. Catchphrase: "It's just... so far away," used in a slightly whiny voice to refer to something he doesn't want to get up to do.Which is most things. Child Hater: Though not to the extent ofMrs. Chesterfield. Deadpan Snarker: Of theServile Snarkervariety, as the family's sarcastic butler.

characters.name: Mr(s). Kipling
characters.description: Action Pet: To an extent. Kipling has saved Christmas decorations, taken care of venomous spiders, andsmacked people with her tail. Cross-Cast Role: Kipling turns out to be female, but is played by a male Asian water monitor lizard named Frank. Reptiles Are Abhorrent: Subverted. Mrs. Kipling seems to be bad, until you get to know her. Samus Is a Girl: Mr. Kipling is really aMrs.Kipling, as revealed in the Season 1 finale. Team Pet: The Ross family's house pet.

characters.name: Tony Ciccolini
characters.description: Crazy Jealous Guy: A downplayed version, but "Green-EyedMonsters" shows that he gets easily jealous over Jessie being friends with Officer Petey. The Ditz: Tony is a slight airhead, but still really funny. Dogged Nice Guy: Tony has been helping Jessie in any way possible throughout the show. Knows a Guy Who Knows a Guy: In one episode, Zuri mentioned that "Tony knows a guy" with regards to bringing in a Porta-potty to her room to help Jessie overcome her fear. Love Interest: To Jessie.They ended the series as a couple.

characters.name: Mrs. Rhoda Chesterfield
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: She has a crush on Bertram, although he is repulsed by her. Abusive Mom: Is shown to be somewhat abusive towards her daughter, Cassandra,Played for Laughsof course. Accidental Misnaming: She always refers to Jessie as names that rhyme with her actual name, like "Bessie" and "Tressie", and she calls Emma "Jemma". She never bothers to remember Jessie’s name no matter how many times she hears it.Malicious Misnaming: Although she might be doing it on purpose to annoy Jessie. Malicious Misnaming: Although she might be doing it on purpose to annoy Jessie. Arch-Enemy:Thearchenemy to Jessie. She has it out for Jessie for no reason, trying to put her down and get rid of her.

characters.name: Morgan and Christina Ross
characters.description: And Starring: "Charles Esten as Morgan Ross". Bumbling Dad: Morgan. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Only Christina returns home in the finale, and Morgan is nowhere to be seen after "Gotcha Day", which is only the 25th episode of the show (which lasted 101 episodes). Disproportionate Retribution: Christina once fired a nanny simply because the nanny was wearing plaid. Follow in My Footsteps: In Bunk'd, it's mentioned Christina sent Emma to camp Kikiwaka in the hopes that Emma will live up to be a great counselor like she was. In turn, Emma WANTS to make her proud, but doesn't feel she can live up to that dream.

characters.name: Nanny Agatha
characters.description: Big Bad Ensemble: One of the recurring antagonists along with Mrs. Chesterfield. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: Her last appearance is in "Pain in the Rear Window", which is only the 38th episode of the show, which made 101 episodes. Even Evil Has Standards: She's a big-time bitch, yes, but even she finds her twin sister Angela despicable. Evil Brit: An ugly and arrogant British nanny. Evil Counterpart: To Jessie, where as Jessie is sweet and compassionate and doesn't try to compete with other Nannies, Agatha is cruel and always down for a competition.

characters.name: "Creepy" Connie Thompson
characters.description: And Now You Must Marry Me: In her third appearance, she tries to force Luke to marry her. Complete with Luke getting dragged to the "altar" while bound in a wheelbarrow lift. Ax-Crazy: While in her first appearance, she is much more creepy than crazy, she is full blownAx-CrazyStalker with a Crushin her next appearance. In "Creepy Connie 3: The Creepening", Connie hires an actress in order to dupe Luke. In order to avoid paying said actress, Conniekidnaps her. Connie herself confirmed she's had at least one police line-up. And her uncle confirms her multiple(!) restraining orders in "Creepy Connie 3" Breakout Villain: She only appears in three episodes but she's easily one of the most popular characters on the show. Clingy Jealous Girl: She wants Luke all to herself and she sees Jessie as competition that she has to get rid of, even though Jessie has made clear she has no feelings for Luke. Creepy Child: She has no qualms with injuring people or tying people up to get what she wants. She's called "Creepy" Connie for a reason...

characters.name: Officer Petey
characters.description: Birds of a Feather: A platonic version with Jessie. Chuck Cunningham Syndrome: His last appearance is in "Pain in the Rear Window", which is only the 38th episode of the show, which made 101 episodes. However, he does get mentioned in some episodes after that. Cloudcuckoolander: He is known for his improv obsession. Only Known by Their Nickname Platonic Life-Partners: According toDebby Ryan, Petey is Jessie's best friend.

characters.name: Stewart Wooten
characters.description: Abhorrent Admirer: For Zuri, until he proved himself not to be useless after all. Dogged Nice Guy: Towards Zuri. He sends her flowers, asks her out on dates, and calls her by sweet nicknames. Nice Guy: He’s nice and polite, even if he acts like a stalker to the girl he likes. Spear Counterpart: Is basically Creepy Connie without the strength, the psychopathy, or theHarry Potterobsession. Stalker with a Crush: He has a huge crush on Zuri. He briefly becomes this to Emma, when he pretends to fall in love with her in order to make Zuri jealous.

characters.name: Rosie
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: She has a cynical sense of humor. Green-Eyed Monster: Admits to Emma that herKick the Dogmoment was an act of jealousy over Emma being beautiful and rich. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: She feels bad when she realizes how herKick the Dogmoment affected Emma and comes to the penthouse to apologize. Kick the Dog: She gets Emma to help her with a project, but intentionally makes her look stupid in front of the class. New Transfer Student: She begins attending Emma's school in "Make New Friends, but Hide The Old".

characters.name: Shaylee Michaels
characters.description: Awesome Aussie: When she was a child, she played outside in the outback and used a live scorpion as a pacifier. As an adult, she wrestles crocodiles/monitor lizards for fun, can terrify Mrs Kipling by hissing and when the monitor lizard bites her, she bites back! She also knows how to make her breath toxic enough to render a person unconscious. The Bus Came Back: Shaylee makes her first appearance in the "Jessie’s Big Break" two-parter. After being absent for 42 episodes, she finally returns in the "Jessie’s Aloha-Holidays with Parker and Joey" two-parter. Famed in Story:In-Universe, she’s a famous actress. Massive Numbered Siblings: She has eight brothers. Raised by Dudes: Shaylee comments that she built up her strength to be just as formidable as her brothers.

characters.name: Bryn Breitbart
characters.description: Alpha Bitch: A snobby, mean girl at Emma's school. Blatant Lies: She tells people she's Danish royalty. She doesn't even have a European accent.Her parents actually run a toilet paper empire. For the Evulz: Basically why Bryn was trying to turn Emma's friends against her. Green-Eyed Monster: She's jealous of Emma because of her status and popularity. Heel–Face Turn:Subvertedin "Diary of A Mad Newswoman":It was all an act to take over the school news broadcast.

characters.name: Angela
characters.description: Beauty Is Bad: She's more attractive than Agatha, but in terms of personality, she isn't any better. Bitch in Sheep's Clothing: Appears as a kind, doting nanny, the opposite of Agatha...useful to get the kids to prefer her over Jessie. Evil Brit: Since she's Agatha's twin after all. Evil Twin: Well... eviler anyway.Even Agatha finds her despicable. Evil Versus Evil: With her twin sister Agatha.

characters.name: Nana Banana
characters.description: Intergenerational Friendship: With Zuri.
